title: SSC CGL 2026 Sectional Timing Tier I 40 40

questions.question: Choose the address that is exactly the same as the one given below. Flat No. 6C, Orchid Heights, Sector 49, Gurugram, Haryana - 122018
options: Flat No. 6C, Orchid Heights, Sector 94, Gurugram, Haryana - 122018
Flat No. 6C, Orchid Heights, Sector 49, Gurugram, Haryana - 122018
Flat No. 6C, Orchid Heights, Sector 49, Gurugram, Haryana - 122108
Flat No. 6C, Orchid Height, Sector 49, Gurugram, Haryana - 122018
questions.explanation: Option b, is identical to the given address in flat number, building name, sector, city, state, and PIN. Option a, changes the sector (94 instead of 49). Option c, changes the PIN code (122108 instead of 122018). Option d, changes the building name (Heights vs Height).

questions.question: Consider the following statement and choose the option that follows. Problem: The number of stray cattle entering cultivated fields has increased in a farming block. Course of Action: 1) Ask farmers to stop growing crops near village boundaries. 2) Direct local authorities to create shelters and strengthen boundary fencing in vulnerable areas.
questions.explanation: Course of Action 1 does not follow because asking farmers to stop cultivation is unreasonable and unfair. Course of Action 2 follows because shelters and fencing can directly reduce the entry of stray cattle into fields. A valid course of action must be practical and protective in nature. Therefore, only 2 follows.

questions.question: Out of the following five figures, four are alike in some manner and one differs from these in that manner. Select the odd figure. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22027/image032.jpg)
questions.explanation: Figure (IV) is different from all the figures - ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22027/image033.png) In all other figures, the mirror image of letters is written whereas in Figure (IV) water image of the letter is written.

questions.question: Select the set of numbers from the options which is similar to the following set of numbers. (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g., 13 - Operations n 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed). (42, 33, 18)
questions.explanation: The logic is: 42 - 33 = 9; 9 * 2 = 18 Similarly, in option b: 57 - 53 = 4; 4 * 2 = 8

questions.question: The sequence of folding a piece of paper and the manner in which the folded paper has been cut is shown in the following figures. Select the option that would most clearly resemble the unfolded form of paper. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25664/image022.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25664/image023.png)
questions.explanation: Figure II would resemble the correct form of a paper after cutting. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25664/image024.png) Thus, the answer is option (b).

questions.question: Which of the following pairs of numbers and signs, when interchanged, will make the equation 20 x 100 - 5 + 63 ÷ 7 = 9 valid?
questions.explanation: After swapping 100 and 20, and x with -: 100 - 20 x 5 + 63 ÷ 7 20 x 5 = 100 63 ÷ 7 = 9 LHS = 100 - 100 + 9 = 9 = RHS

questions.question: In how many ways all the letters of the word THUNDERSTORM be arranged such that all vowels are together?
questions.explanation: Consonants in the word THUNDERSTORM = T, H, N, D, R, S, T, R, M Vowels in the word THUNDERSTORM = U, E, O Total number of ways = 10! * 3!/2!*2! = 15* 9!

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the fifth term in the same way as the second term is related to the first term and the fourth term is related to the third term. BACKDRAFT : TCADKFARB :: EQUIVOCAL : LUQVIACOE :: HYPNOTIZE : ?
questions.explanation: BACKDRAFT : TCADKFARB ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p24388/image005.png) EQUIVOCAL : LUQVIACOE ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p24388/image006.png) Similarly, ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p24388/image007.png) Thus, the answer is option (c).

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the fifth term in the same way as the second term is related to the first term and the fourth term is related to the third term. 1_________32H : 1_________40 :: 1_________50E : 1_________55 :: 1_________43K : ?
questions.explanation: Here, the pattern is: 32H = 32+8 (where 8 is the place value of H) = 40 50E = 50+5 (where 5 is the place value of E) = 55 43K = 43+11 (where 11 is the place value of K) = 54

questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series based on the English alphabetical order? C, EG, IKM, OQSU, ?
questions.explanation: Here, the pattern is: 1ˢᵗ term contains 1 alphabet. 2ⁿᵈ term contains 2 alphabets. 3ʳᵈ term contains 3 alphabets. 4ᵗʰ term contains 4 alphabets. Thus, the next term will contain 5 alphabets. C+2= E; E+2= G; G+2= I; I+2= K; K+2= M; M+2= O; O+2= Q; Q+2= S; S+2= U. So, U+2= W; W+2= Y; Y+2= A; A+2= C; C+2= E

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third word in the same way as the second word is related to the first word. (The words must be considered as meaningful English word and must not be related to each other based on the number of letters/ numbers of consonants/ vowels in the word) Uttar Pradesh : Dudhwa National Park :: Himachal Pradesh : ?
questions.explanation: Dudhwa National Park is in Uttar Pradesh. In the same way, Pin Valley National Park is in Himachal Pradesh.

questions.question: In a certain language, 'fast track the performance' is coded as 'te tk pe ft', 'your performance has deteriorated' is coded as 'dd ke hs tk' and 'track the package' is coded as 'yr ft te'. What could be the code for 'deteriorated fast'?
questions.explanation: 'fast track the performance' as 'te tk pe ft' .. (i) 'your performance has deteriorated' as 'dd ke hs tk' .. (ii) 'track the package' as 'yr ft te' .. (iii) From (i) and (iii), track/ the is ft/ te From (i) and (ii), performance is tk From (i), Fast is pe From (iii), Your/ has/ deteriorated is ke/ dd/ hs So, the code for 'deteriorated fast' is 'ke/ dd/ hs pe' and we have 'hs pe' in option. Hence, the code for 'deteriorated fast' is 'hs pe'.

questions.question: In the question given below, five statements are given followed by three conclusions. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts. Read the conclusions and decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the given statements disregarding the commonly known facts. Statements: Some buildings are auditorium. All halls are buildings. No cineplex is auditorium. Some cafeteria are halls. Only a few cafeteria are not stadium. Conclusion: I. Some stadium is hall is a possibility. II. Some buildings are cafeteria. III. Some buildings are cineplex.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p15894/image010.jpg) Some stadium is hall is a possibility: All stadium can be cafeteria and some cafeteria are halls. So, conclusion I is true. Some buildings are cafeteria: This conclusion is true. Some buildings are cineplex: There is no definite relation between building and cineplex. So, conclusion III is false.

questions.question: Sana is introducing a person and she says, " The person is the only daughter of the only son of her spouse". How is Sana(who is a female) related to the person?
questions.explanation: Sana's and her spouse have only one son who has only daughter. Hence, the person is Sana's granddaughter and Sana is her grandmother.

questions.question: Select the figure that will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following figure series. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p24375/image012.png)
questions.explanation: The logic is given below: Movements of the letters are shown by the arrow, and upper right corner letter is changing to its previous letter according to English alphabet & bottom left corner letter is changing to its immediate next letter according to English alphabet. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p24375/image013.png) So, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which of the following numbers will replace the question mark (?) in the given series? 38, 40, 83, 253, ?, 5091, 30553
questions.explanation: 38 x 1 + 2 = 40 40 x 2 + 3 = 83 83 x 3 + 4 = 253 253 x 4 + 5 = 1017 1017 x 5 + 6 = 5091 5091 x 6 + 7 = 30553 So, '1017' will replace the question mark (?) in the given series.

questions.question: Select the option that represents the letters, symbols and digits which, when sequentially placed from left to right in the blanks below, will complete the letter series. P_2K_ _&_K∞P_2_∞P_2K_
questions.explanation: Option (a) P&2K∞P&2K∞P&2K∞P&2K∞ Here, the pattern 'P&2K∞' is repeating. So, option (a) is correct. Option (b) P&2K&∞&PK∞P22K∞PK2K& Here, no definite pattern can be established. So, option (b) is incorrect. Option (c) PK2K&2&PK∞P222∞PK2K& Here, no definite pattern can be established. So, option (c) is incorrect. Option (d) P&2K2P&2K∞P&2K∞P&2KK Here, no definite pattern can be established. So, option (d) is incorrect.

questions.question: Study the following question and answer the given question. A 1 B means A is B's son, A 2 B means A is B's brother, A 3 B means A is B's sister, A 4 B means A is B's mother. If M 3 X 4 Y 2 L 1 N, then how is M related to N?
questions.explanation: It is given that, M 3 X 4 Y 2 L 1 N It means that, M is X's sister. X is Y's mother. Y is L's brother. L is N's son. We get, ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p15424/image003.png) Here, M is the sister-in-law of N.

questions.question: Study the given pattern carefully and select the number that can replace the question mark(?) in it. (12,134),(14,186),(17,?)
questions.explanation: The second number in each pair is 10 less than the square of the first number. So, 17²=289-10=279

questions.question: There is a crisis and you are the only person with a vehicle, which is a two-wheeler, at the place. Four persons are going through some health emergencies/issues. Who among the following should you take to the hospital?
options: A pregnant woman who is feeling nauseous
A man who is bleeding due to an accidental cut on his arm by a rock protrusion which would require stitches
A boy with epilepsy suffering an epileptic seizure
A woman with a respiratory disease having mild difficulty in breathing
questions.explanation: Both the women should be taken to an area (in the vicinity) where it is not crowded and they can breathe some fresh air. For the woman with the respiratory disease, since it is a mild (and not heavy) difficulty in breathing, so going to the hospital is not necessary. The first aid for seizures is aimed at keeping the person safe until the seizure stops on its own. So, the boy with the seizure also does not require to be sent to the hospital. Hence, the man who requires stitches should be the one who needs to see a doctor as soon as possible.

questions.question: Find the missing term in the below series? 2518 8103 528 4225?
questions.explanation: Column -1; (8 X 5) + 2 = 42 Column -2; (10 X 2) + 5 = 25 In the same way missing term in column 3 = (8 X 3) + 18 = 42

questions.question: Which word, when its vowels are removed, yields consonants that are in alphabetical order?
questions.explanation: Remove vowels from BLOOM → B L M Check B < L < M Hence BLOOM is correct.

questions.question: Read the given statement and conclusions carefully. Decide which of the given conclusions is/are definitely true from the statement. Statement: F > B < E ≤ C = D ≥ A > G Conclusion I: G < F Conclusion II: C > G
options: Both Conclusion I and Conclusion II are true
Neither Conclusion I nor Conclusion II is true
questions.explanation: Statement: F > B < E ≤ C = D ≥ A > G I: G < F does not follow as relation between G and F cannot be determined. II: C > G follows as F > B < E ≤ C = D ≥ A > G Hence, only conclusion II true.

questions.question: Compare the following two issue tracker tickets. Record 1: Ticket: PROJ-1478, Title: "Login timeout after OTP", Priority: P2, Reporter: S. Mehta, Created: 02-09-2025 11:08 Record 2: Ticket: PROJ-1479, Title: Login timeout after OTP, Priority: P2, Reporter: S. Mehta, Created: 02/09/2025 11:08 Are these likely to be the same?
options: No, the ticket IDs are different
questions.explanation: Title, priority, reporter, and timestamp align after normalization. Ticket key differs: PROJ-1478 vs PROJ-1479. Ticket ID uniquely identifies the case; therefore not the same.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Kanva Dynasty: 1.The Kanva dynasty was founded by Vasudeva Kanva. 2.The Kanvas overthrew the last ruler of the Shunga dynasty. 3.The Kanva rulers were eventually replaced by the Satavahanas. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: The Kanva Dynasty (c. 73 BCE - 28 BCE) was a short-lived Brahmin dynasty that ruled Magadha after the fall of the Shungas. Statement 1: Founder (Correct) The dynasty was founded by Vasudeva Kanva. He was a Brahmin who belonged to the Kanva gotra, which gave the dynasty its name. Statement 2: Overthrow of the Shungas (Correct) Vasudeva Kanva was originally a minister (Amatya) in the court of the last Shunga King, Devabhuti. According to the Puranas and Bana's Harshacharita, Vasudeva orchestrated a conspiracy and assassinated the weak Devabhuti to usurp the throne, effectively ending Shunga rule in Magadha. Statement 3: Replacement by the Satavahanas (Correct) The Kanva dynasty consisted of only four rulers: Vasudeva, Bhumimitra, Narayana, and Susharman. The last king, Susharman, was killed around 28 BCE by the Satavahana (also known as Andhra) king, Simuka. With this, the political center of gravity shifted from Magadha (North India) to the Deccan (Central/South India).

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the book Unhappy India: Statement I: Unhappy India was written by Lala Lajpat Rai. Statement II: The book was written as a response to the Simon Commission. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: I. is Correct. Unhappy India was written by the prominent nationalist leader Lala Lajpat Rai and published in 1928. II. is Correct. The book was written as a stinging rebuttal to Katherine Mayo's Mother India and served as a nationalist critique of British policies and the Simon Commission, which was visiting India at the time. Further Insights: Lala Lajpat Rai is also known as 'Punjab Kesari' (Lion of Punjab).

questions.question: The book "Anandamath" is primarily associated with which theme?
options: The rise of Indian nationalism and anti-colonial resistance
questions.explanation: · Anandamath by Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay revolves around ascetic revolutionaries, the Santhal rebellion background, and the emergence of nationalist consciousness. · The novel also introduced "Vande Mataram", later becoming a national song.

questions.question: In the photoelectric effect, the kinetic energy of emitted electrons depends on:
questions.explanation: The kinetic energy of emitted electrons depends on the frequency of the incident radiation. According to Einstein's photoelectric equation ( E=hν−Φ), the maximum kinetic energy of an electron is directly proportional to the frequency (ν) of the light. The Intensity of light determines the "number" of electrons emitted (current), but not their energy. Further Insights: If the frequency is below a certain "threshold frequency," no electrons are emitted regardless of the light's intensity.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding capital expenditure: 1. It leads to the creation of durable assets. 2. It has a direct impact on fiscal deficit. 3. It includes interest payments on government debt. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. Statement 3 is incorrect because interest payments are categorized as revenue expenditure. Explanation of Statements: 1) is Correct. Capital expenditure (CapEx) involves spending on the creation of physical assets like roads, bridges, and machinery, or the acquisition of land. 2) is Correct. Since CapEx is a part of total expenditure, it directly impacts the fiscal deficit (the gap between total expenditure and non-debt receipts). 3) is Incorrect. Interest payments do not create assets or reduce liabilities; they are recurring "maintenance" costs of debt and are thus classified as Revenue Expenditure. Further Insights: High CapEx is generally viewed positively for long-term growth because of its high "multiplier effect" on the economy.

questions.question: Select the option that is true regarding the following two statements labelled Assertion(A) and Reason(R) Assertion (A): The Law of Diminishing Marginal Product states that when additional units of a variable factor are added to fixed factors, the marginal product of the variable factor eventually declines. Reason (R): In the short run, at least one factor of production remains fixed, which leads to inefficiencies when more variable factors are employed.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true, but R is false
A is false, but R is true
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) is true · The law applies in the short run, where output initially rises at an increasing rate, then at a decreasing rate as marginal product falls. Reason (R) is true · Since at least one factor (e.g., land or capital) is fixed, additional units of the variable factor (e.g., labour) face: o Overcrowding o Poor factor coordination · This leads to declining efficiency and hence diminishing marginal product. Further insights · The law operates only in the short run. · It applies to all factors of production, not just labour. · Marginal product may initially increase due to better division of labour. · Diminishing returns set in due to factor indivisibility and congestion. · The law underpins the downward-sloping marginal product curve.

questions.question: Which of the following components of the Chandrayaan-3 mission was specifically responsible for achieving a soft landing on the Moon?
questions.explanation: The Vikram Lander was responsible for executing the powered descent and soft landing on the lunar surface using its onboard sensors, throttleable engines, and autonomous navigation system. Propulsion Module: Carried the lander and rover from Earth orbit to lunar orbit. Pragyan Rover: Conducted surface mobility and in-situ scientific experiments. LVM3: Launched the mission from Earth. Chandrayaan-3 is a follow-on mission to Chandrayaan-2 to demonstrate end-to-end capability in safe landing and roving on the lunar surface. It consists of Lander and Rover configuration. It was launched by LVM3 from SDSC SHAR, Sriharikota.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about glacial till and till plains is correct?
options: Rock fragments in glacial till are generally rounded due to prolonged water transport.
Glacial till is also known as Boulder Clay and consists of large boulders embedded in a fine-grained matrix of clay or silt.
Basal till is deposited from the top of the glacier during melting, while ablation till is lodged beneath the glacier due to pressure.
Till plains are generally infertile regions due to poor soil development and lack of minerals.
questions.explanation: Glacial till is commonly referred to as "Boulder Clay". · Large erratic boulders · Embedded within a fine-grained clay or silt matrix · Glacial till is deposited directly by ice, not by running water. · Due to minimal transport, rock fragments remain angular and sharp-edged, not rounded. The statement reverses the correct classification: · Basal till → deposited beneath the glacier due to pressure. · Ablation till → deposited from the surface as ice melts. Till plains are typically: · Low-lying and gently undulating · Composed of mineral-rich glacial deposits · they often develop into fertile agricultural regions. Further Insights · Glacial till is unsorted and unstratified material. · Angular clasts indicate direct ice deposition. · Till plains record past continental glaciation. · Fertile till soils support intensive agriculture in mid-latitudes.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding Article 280 of the Indian Constitution: 1) One of the core mandates of Article 280 is to recommend the distribution of the net proceeds of taxes between the Union and the States. 2) Article 280(3)(b) requires the Commission to state the principles governing Grants-in-Aid of the revenues of the States out of the Consolidated Fund of India. 3) The recommendations made by the Finance Commission under Article 280 are advisory in nature and not legally binding on the Government of India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 - Correct · Article 280 provides for the constitution of the Finance Commission. · A primary function is to recommend the vertical devolution of taxes between the Union and the States. · This ensures fiscal federalism and balanced regional development. Statement 2 - Correct · Article 280(3)(b) explicitly mandates the Finance Commission to recommend: The principles governing Grants-in-Aid to States These grants are charged on the Consolidated Fund of India · This provision supports fiscally weaker States. Statement 3 - Correct · Finance Commission recommendations are not legally enforceable. · They are advisory, but: o Carry high constitutional authority o Are generally accepted by the Union Government Further Insights: · The Finance Commission is a constitutional body, unlike the GST Council (constitutional but different mandate). · It addresses both vertical and horizontal fiscal imbalances. · Grants-in-Aid help States with revenue deficits and special needs. · The President constitutes the Finance Commission every five years. · The Finance Commission strengthens cooperative federalism in India.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding mineral deposits in India: Statement I: Odisha is the leading producer of chromite in India. Statement II: Iron ore deposits of Bailadila are located in Jharkhand. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement I: Chromite Production (Correct) Odisha holds a near-monopoly on chromite production in India. It accounts for over 95% of India's chromite resources and production. The most significant deposits are found in the Sukinda Valley in the Jajpur district. Chromite is an essential mineral used primarily in the production of stainless steel and various alloys. Statement II: Bailadila Iron Ore (Incorrect) The Bailadila iron ore deposits are not located in Jharkhand; they are situated in the Dantewada district of Chhattisgarh. Significance: Bailadila is famous for its very high-grade hematite iron ore. Infrastructure: A significant portion of the iron ore from Bailadila is exported to Japan and South Korea via the Visakhapatnam port. Jharkhand's Deposits: Jharkhand is indeed rich in iron ore, but its major mines are located in areas like Noamundi, Gua, and Kiriburu (Singhbhum district).

questions.question: In October 2025, who was honoured as a 'Top Agri-Food Pioneer' by the World Food Prize Foundation, held in Des Moines, Iowa?
questions.explanation: Sanjeev Kapoor has been honoured as a Top Agri-Food Pioneer by the World Food Prize Foundation. The ceremony was held in Des Moines, Iowa. The event brings together global leaders, scientists, policy makers, and innovators to discuss key issues related to food security, climate resilience, and nutrition. The recognition marks Kapoor's transformative contribution to improving the global food ecosystem by promoting healthy and sustainable eating, supporting Indian agriculture, and encouraging the use of local, nutrient-rich ingredients. The award was presented during the Borlaug Dialogue Week, hosted annually by the World Food Prize Foundation.

questions.question: Match the following Articles with their related provisions: List I (Article)List II (Provision) A. Article 300A1. Right to property B. Article 3612. Immunities of President and Governor C. Article 2623. Adjudication of inter-State water disputes D. Article 323A4. Administrative Tribunals Codes:
questions.explanation: · Article 300A - Guarantees the Right to Property as a constitutional (not fundamental) right. · Article 361 - Provides immunity to the President and Governors from criminal proceedings during their term of office. · Article 262 - Deals with adjudication of disputes relating to inter-State river waters. · Article 323A - Empowers Parliament to establish Administrative Tribunals for public service matters.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of the 'Pradhan Mantri Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana' launched by the Government of India in 2024?
options: To provide free electricity to all rural households through the expansion of coal-based thermal power plants.
To promote rooftop solar installations in 1 crore households, providing up to 300 units of free electricity per month.
To subsidize the purchase of electric vehicles (EVs) for urban middle-class families to reduce carbon emissions.
To establish large-scale solar parks in desert regions to power industrial corridors exclusively.
questions.explanation: The Pradhan Mantri Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana aims to: · Install rooftop solar panels in 1 crore households · Provide up to 300 units of free electricity per month · Reduce household electricity bills and dependence on grid power · It also promotes decentralised solar energy and citizen participation in energy transition. Further Insights · The scheme promotes distributed solar power generation. · It supports India's target of net-zero emissions by 2070. · Rooftop solar reduces transmission losses. · The scheme enhances energy security at the household level. · It complements India's broader renewable energy capacity expansion goals.

questions.question: Bardo chham is a folk dance of which Indian state?
questions.explanation: Bardo chham is a folk dance of Arunachal Pradesh. Assam- Sattriya Manipur - Manipuri

questions.question: Cheiraoba, the lunar new year in Manipur, is celebrated in the month of
questions.explanation: Cheiraoba is considered one of the most important festivals of Manipur. It is celebrated with great pomp and joy, especially, by the Meiteis of the state. Usually celebrated in the month of April, it marks the beginning of the lunar new year for the Meiteis residing in Manipur and elsewhere. While the followers of Sanamahism, Meitei's indigenous religion, celebrate it on the first day of the lunar new year, the followers of Vaishnav Hindus, celebrate it on the 13th or 14th of April every year according to the solar Hindu calendar.

questions.question: Match the Constitutional Schedule with the content List IList II 1) First Schedulea. Languages recognized by the Constitution. 2) Seventh Scheduleb. Names and territorial extent of States and Union Territories. 3) Eighth Schedulec. Division of powers into Union, State and Concurrent Lists. 4) Tenth Scheduled. Anti-defection law.
questions.explanation: First Schedule lists States/UTs and their territories. Seventh Schedule distributes legislative subjects across three lists. Eighth Schedule enumerates recognized languages. Tenth Schedule contains the anti-defection provisions. Further Insights: - The Eighth Schedule currently lists 22 languages (last added: Bodo, Dogri, Maithili, Santali in 2003). - Anti-defection law (Tenth Schedule, 1985; strengthened by 91st Amendment, 2003) sets disqualification for defection. - Union, State, Concurrent Lists contain 97, 66, and 47 subjects respectively originally; counts have changed over time. - First Schedule also includes the list of UTs and outlines territorial changes after reorganization. - Constitutional Schedules now total 12 (Twelfth added by 74th Amendment for municipalities).

questions.question: In tennis, what is the term for a score of 40-40, indicating a tied game?
questions.explanation: In tennis, when the score is tied at 40-40, it is referred to as "Deuce." This means that both players or teams have won three points each in that game, and they must win by two consecutive points to win the game.

questions.question: Justice Surya Kant took the oath as the 53rd Chief Justice of India (CJI), in November 2025. As per the official communication, he is expected to serve until ____________.
questions.explanation: Justice Surya Kant took the oath as the 53rd Chief Justice of India (CJI). Justice Kant was recommended for the position by outgoing chief justice of India Bhushan Ramkrishna Gavai, whose term ended on November 23, 2025. Last month, the Law and Justice Ministry had asked Justice Gavai to suggest a name for the next Chief Justice. According to the existing set of rules for appointing judges, the senior-most Supreme Court judge who is fit for the position is recommended as the next CJI. In line with the rules, Kant, who was the senior-most judge after Justice Gavai, was recommended. Kant is expected to serve as the Chief Justice of India until February 9, 2027. He became the Chief Justice of the Himachal Pradesh High Court on October 5, 2018, and was later elevated to the Supreme Court of India on May 24, 2019. Currently, he also serves as the Chairman of the Supreme Court Legal Services Committee (since November 12, 2024).

questions.question: Which of the following statements are correct? I. Simon Commission (1927) had no Indian members and later recommended provincial autonomy. II. Cripps Mission (1942) proposed immediate transfer of power to an Interim National Government. III. Poona Pact (1932) replaced separate electorates for the Depressed Classes with reserved seats in joint electorates.
questions.explanation: I is correct: boycott arose due to all-British composition; provincial autonomy was among recommendations. II is incorrect: it offered a Constituent Assembly and post-war Dominion status, not immediate transfer. III is correct: it provided reserved seats in joint electorates with more seats than in the Communal Award. Further Insights: - The Nehru Report (1928) proposed Dominion Status and joint electorates. - The 1929 Lahore session declared Purna Swaraj; 26 January observed as Independence Day. - August Offer (1940) and Wavell Plan (1945) preceded Cabinet Mission (1946). - Cabinet Mission Plan proposed a Union with limited subjects and groupings of provinces.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding Mitosis: 1.It results in two genetically identical daughter cells. 2.It occurs in somatic cells. 3.It involves reduction of chromosome number. 4.It plays a role in growth and tissue repair. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Mitosis is a type of cell division that results in two daughter cells each having the same number and kind of chromosomes as the parent nucleus. 1. Genetically Identical Daughter Cells (Correct): Mitosis is an "equational division." The DNA is replicated and then distributed equally, ensuring that both daughter cells are clones of the parent cell. 2. Occurs in Somatic Cells (Correct): Somatic cells are the "body" cells (like skin, bone, and blood cells). Mitosis is the standard process for these cells to multiply. In contrast, Meiosis occurs in germ cells to produce gametes (sperm and egg). 3. Reduction of Chromosome Number (Incorrect): This is a characteristic of Meiosis, not Mitosis. In Mitosis, the chromosome number remains the same (diploid to diploid). In Meiosis, the number is halved (diploid to haploid). 4. Role in Growth and Tissue Repair (Correct): Since mitosis produces identical cells, it is the primary mechanism for increasing the size of an organism (growth) and replacing damaged or dead cells (repair). The Stages of Mitosis: Mitosis is a continuous process but is generally divided into four main stages: Prophase: Chromosomes condense and the nuclear envelope breaks down. Metaphase: Chromosomes line up at the cell's equator (metaphase plate). Anaphase: Sister chromatids are pulled apart to opposite poles. Telophase: Two new nuclear envelopes form around the separated chromosomes.

questions.question: The song 'Jana-Gana-Mana' composed by Rabindranath Tagore was first published in January 1912 under the title of________.
questions.explanation: Written in Bengali, the first of five stanzas of the Brahmo hymn titled Bharoto Bhagyo Bidhata attributed to Nobel laureate Rabindranath Tagore. Underlying message of the Jana Gana Mana is pluralism. It was adopted in its Hindi version by the Constituent Assembly as the National Anthem of India on 24 January 1950.

questions.question: Which state created a Guinness World Record by installing 45,911 off-grid solar agricultural pumps in a month under PM-KUSUM (Component B) and Magel Tyala Saur Krushi Pump Yojana?
questions.explanation: Maharashtra government on December 5, 2025 said it had installed 45,911 off-grid solar agricultural pumps in a month under the PM-KUSUM (Component B) and Magel Tyala Saur Krushi Pump Yojana schemes, claiming a world record for it. He was awarded by the Guinness World Records for the initiative of the Maharashtra State Electricity Distribution Company Limited (MSEDCL). Maharashtra has so far installed 7.47 lakh solar pumps in the State. It targets to install 10.45 lakh pumps. The government said that a special focus was given on drought-prone zones for the installation of solar pumps. The pumps were sized on the basis of landholdings. e.g. 3 HP pumps were fitted for up to 2.5 acres of land, 5 HP pumps for up to 5 acres of land.

questions.question: The decline in the proportion of the child population (0 - 6 years) from 15.9% in 2001 to 13.6% in 2011, as recorded by the Census of India, most directly indicates?
options: Rising infant mortality rates across rural India
Increasing life expectancy leading to population ageing
Declining fertility rates accompanied by socio-economic transformation
Large-scale emigration of young families from India
questions.explanation: The decline reflects: · Lower Total Fertility Rate (TFR) · Delayed marriages · Greater access to family planning · Higher female literacy Rising infant mortality would reduce child survival, not the share of children at birth. Increased life expectancy affects the elderly proportion, Further Insights · Declining child population is a proxy indicator of fertility transition. · India's TFR fell close to replacement level (2.1) after 2011. · Lower child dependency improves per capita investment in health and education.

questions.question: Babur fought Battle of Ghaghra in 16ᵗʰ century with which of the following rulers?
questions.explanation: · Battle of Ghaghra was fought in 1529 between Muhammad Lodhi and Babur. · Babur defeated Ibrahim Lodhi in first battle of Panipat in 1526. · Babur defeated Rana Sanga in Battle of Khanwa in 1527. · Babur defeated Medini Rai at Chanderi in 1528.

questions.question: Match the following Ramsar Sites declared in 2025 with their respective States: List- I (Ramsar Site) List - II (State) A. Gokul Jalashay 1. Rajasthan B. Menar Wetland Complex 2. Chhattisgarh C. Kopra Jalashay 3. Bihar D. Therthangal Bird Sanctuary 4. Tamil Nadu
questions.explanation: As of December 2025, India has a total of 96 Ramsar Sites. Gokul Jalashay, a designated Ramsar Site (September 2025) in Buxar district, Bihar, India, is an important oxbow lake of the Ganges River known for migratory birds, blackbuck habitat, and serving as a natural flood buffer, bringing India's total to 93 Ramsar sites. This freshwater wetland supports local livelihoods through fishing and farming and highlights Bihar's growing commitment to wetland conservation alongside Udaipur Jheel. On June 4, 2025 a day before World Environment Day, the Union Environment Ministry announced the designation of two new Ramsar sites in India-Khichan in Phalodi and Menar in Udaipur, Rajasthan. The Kopra Reservoir in Chhattisgarh's Bilaspur district is a significant man-made wetland recently designated(Decemeber 2025) as India's 95th Ramsar Site and the state's first. In January 2025, Tamil Nadu further strengthened its position as India's leading state in wetland conservation with the declaration of two new Ramsar sites - Sakkarakottai Bird Sanctuary and Therthangal Bird Sanctuary, both located in Ramanathapuram district. These additions bring the total number of Ramsar sites in the state to 20, the highest in the country. Ramsar Sites Declared in 2025 State Wetland Site Date of Designation Date/Year of Declaration Bihar Gokul Jalashay 13.05.2025 26.09.2025 Bihar Udaipur Jheel 13.05.2025 26.09.2025 Bihar Gogabil Lake 13.05.2025 30.10.2025 Chhattisgarh Kopra Jalashay 08.08.2025 12.12.2025 Jharkhand Udhwa Lake Bird Sanctuary 08-01-2024 30.01.2025 Rajasthan Menar Wetland Complex 19-02-2025 04.06.2025 Rajasthan Khichan wetland 19-02-2025 04.06.2025 Rajasthan Siliserh Lake 07.07.2025 12.12.2025 Sikkim Khachoedpalri wetland 15-07-2024 30.01.2025 Tamil Nadu Sakkarakottai Bird Sanctuary 15-07-2024 30.01.2025 Tamil Nadu Therthangal Bird Sanctuary 15-07-2024 30.01.2025

questions.question: The cost price and selling price of a book is Rs. X and Rs. Y respectively. If X - Y = Rs.1200 and loss% = 15%, then find the value of Y.
questions.explanation: Given that X - Y = Rs.1200 And loss% = 15% So, 15% = Rs.1200 1% = Rs.80 100% = Rs.8000 Cost price or X = Rs.8000 So, Y = 8000 - 1200 = Rs.6800

questions.question: Ages of A and B after 2 years will be in the ratio 2: 3 respectively, ages of B and C before two years were in the ratio 7: 8 respectively and present ages of A and C are in the ratio 5: 9 respectively. Find the present age of B.
questions.explanation: Let present ages of A, B and C are a, b and c respectively. Ages of A and B after 2 years will be in the ratio 2: 3 respectively, i.e. (a + 2): (b + 2) = 2: 3 a = (2b - 2)/3 Ages of B and C before two years were in the ratio 7: 8 respectively, i.e. (b - 2): (c - 2) = 7: 8 c = (8b - 2)/7 Present ages of A and C are in the ratio 5: 9 respectively, i.e. a: c = 5: 9 9 x (2b - 2)/3 = 5 x (8b - 2)/7 b = 16 years

questions.question: There are some tomatoes in the basket. 22% of them got sold and 18 are rotten and 66% remain unsold in the basket. Find the number of tomatoes in the basket initially.
questions.explanation: Let the total number of tomatoes in the basket = x According to question, x - (x * 22/100 + 18) = 66% of x x - 22% of x - 18 - 66% of x = 0 x - 88% of x = 18 12% of x = 18 x = 150

questions.question: The given pie chart shows information about Non-Performing Assets (NPA) processed by the various banks of India till December 2017. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/rrbgrpd-past2-202208001.png) If the total NPAs under all the banks are worth ₹ 300 lakh crores, then NPAs worth how much (in ₹ lakh crores) does Bank 'D' contain till December 2017?
questions.explanation: Worth of total NPAs under all the banks = ₹300 lakh crores Then, worth of NPAs under bank D = 5% of 300 = ₹15 lakh crore rupees

questions.question: KLMN is a parallelogram such that ∠ MKL = X°, ∠ KML = Y°, and ∠ KNM = Z°. Find the value of (X + Y + Z).
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22067/image004.png) We know that opposite angles of parallelogram are equal. So, ∠ KNM = ∠ KLM = Z° Sum of all angles in a triangle = 180° So, ∠ LKM + ∠ KLM + ∠ LMK = 180° X + Y + Z = 180°

questions.question: What must be added to the numbers 3, 5, 7 and 10 such that they become in proportion in the same order?
questions.explanation: Let the number added be 'x' So, (x + 3)/(x + 5) = (x + 7)/(x + 10) => x² + 12x + 35 = x² + 13x + 30 => x = 5

questions.question: A man covers a total distance of 1560 kms via various modes of transport. He covers one-third distance via train, 220 kms by bus and rest by plane. The train costs him Rs.3 per km and plane costs him Rs.5 per km. If he spent Rs.6364 on his travel, what is the cost per km for travelling by bus:
questions.explanation: Distance travelled by train = (1560/3) = 520 kms Distance travelled by bus = 220 kms Distance travelled using plane = 1560 - 520 - 220 = 820 kms Total expenditure on travelling by bus = 6364 - (520 X 3) - (820 X 5) = Rs.704 Cost per km for travelling by bus = 704/220 = Rs.3.20 per km

questions.question: If the value of ¼.231 is 0.2363, then what is the value of 1/0.0004231?
A car starts with 60km/h and increases its speed by 10km/h after every 2 hours. Find the time (in hours) it will take to complete 500kms.
questions.explanation: Distance travelled in first 2 hrs = 60*2 = 120 Distance travelled in next 2 hrs = 70*2 = 140 Distance travelled in next 2 hrs = 80*2 = 160 Total distance travelled so far = 120 + 140 + 160 = 420km Distance left = 500 - 420 = 80km Speed = 90km/h Time = 80/90 = 8/9 hrs Total time = 6 + 8/9 = 6 8/9 hrs

questions.question: Find the remainder when 2⁵⁰ + 3⁵⁰ is divided by 7.
questions.explanation: 2³ = 8 8 mod 7 = 1 2⁵⁰ = 2⁽³ * ¹⁶ ⁺ ²⁾ 2⁵⁰ mod 7 = (2³)¹⁶ X 2² mod 7 = 1¹⁶ X 4 mod 7 = 4 For 3⁵⁰ mod 7: 3² = 9 9 mod 7 = 2 3⁵⁰ = (3²)²⁵ 3⁵⁰ mod 7 = 2²⁵ mod 7 2³ mod 7 = 1 2²⁵ = 2⁽³ * ⁸ ⁺ ¹⁾ 2²⁵ mod 7 = (2³)⁸ X 2 mod 7 = 1⁸ X 2 mod 7 = 2 So, (2⁵⁰ + 3⁵⁰) mod 7 = 4 + 2 = 6

questions.question: Find the smallest number by which 578 must be multiplied to make it a perfect square.
questions.explanation: Option (a): 578 * 4 = 2312, is not a perfect square Option (b): 578 * 8 = 4624, is a perfect square of 68. Option (c): 578 * 6 = 3468, is not a perfect square Option (d): 578 * 5 = 2890, is not a perfect square. Express method: Factors of 578 = 2 * (17 * 17) Here, 17 makes a pair, we have to find the pair of 2, which makes a perfect square. From options, pair of 2 and 8 makes a perfect square. Hence, option 'b' is correct.

questions.question: Find the least 4-digit number which leaves a remainder of 11 when divided by 12, 18 and 20.
questions.explanation: Least number leaving the same remainder on division by given numbers = (least common multiple of those numbers X a suitable integer) + common remainder Let L = least common multiple of 12, 18 and 20. 12 = 2² X 3 18 = 2 X 3² 20 = 2² X 5 L = 2² X 3² X 5 L = 180 Required number = (180 X k) + 11, where k is the least integer making the number 4-digit 180 X 5 + 11 = 911 (not a 4-digit number) 180 X 6 + 11 = 1091 Least 4-digit required number = 1091

questions.question: Find the value of y, if roots of the quadratic equation p² - 18p + 3y = 0 are real and equal.
questions.explanation: p² - 18p + 3y = 0 a = 1, b = -18 and c = 3y If the equation has equal roots, then - b² - 4ac = 0 (-18)² - 4 * 1 * 3y = 0 324 - 12y = 0 12y = 324 y = 27

questions.question: If 6 * P's Capital = 8 * Q's Capital = 9 * R's Capital then out of the total profit of Rs. 4350, Q will receive:

questions.explanation: The base area of box = 72 sq. cm Height of the box = Volume of the box/Base area = 792/72 = 11 cm

questions.question: Rs. X amounts to 54% more than Rs. X in 2 years at simple interest. Find the rate of interest.
questions.explanation: Simple interest = 54% of X = Rs. (54X/100) Rate of Interest = [(54X/100) * 100]/ (X * 2) = 27%

questions.question: The non-terminating repeating decimal among the following fractions is:
questions.explanation: [If the denominator contains only factors of 2 and/or 5, then its decimal expansion will terminate] (a)331/28 Factor of 28 = 2 * 2 * 7 [non-terminating decimal] (b)389/40 Factor of 40 = 2 * 2 * 2 * 5 [Terminating decimal] (c)347/8 Factor of 8 = 2 * 2 * 2 [Terminating decimal] (d)377/16 Factor off 16 = 2 * 2 * 2 * 2 [Terminating decimal]

questions.question: Each exterior angle of a regular polygon is 24°, then how many sides the regular polygon has?
questions.explanation: For a polygon having N sides, Each exterior angle = (360°/N) 24° = (360°/N) N = (360°/24°) = 15.

questions.question: A solid hemisphere is made by melting 64 solid hemispheres of radius 20 cm. Which of the following statements is/are true? A) The diameter of the new hemisphere is 160 cm. B) 50% more than the radius of new hemisphere is 120 cm.
questions.explanation: The volume of the new hemisphere = 64*volume of the hemisphere 2πr³/3 = 64* 2π*20*20*20/3 r³ = 64*8000 r = 4*20 = 80 cm Statement A: Diameter of the new hemisphere = 2*80 = 160 cm Hence A is true. Statement B: 80 x 150/100 = 120 cm Hence B is true.

questions.question: Choose the synonym of 'intrepid':
questions.explanation: Intrepid - fearless; adventurous. Sissy - feeble and cowardly; feeble - lacking strength of character; spineless - weak and purposeless; audacious - showing a willingness to take surprisingly bold risks. Thus, 'audacious' is the only word that can be a synonym of the word 'intrepid'. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Resist
questions.explanation: 'Resist' means to withstand or oppose something; to refuse to give in to a force, temptation, or influence. 'Imbibe' means to drink or absorb something, especially liquids such as alcohol; to take in or assimilate knowledge, ideas, or influences. So, the two words can be used as antonyms of each other. Hence, (d) is the correct answer. Remind: To cause someone to remember something or to bring something back to their awareness; to prompt recollection or memory. Partake: To participate in or share in something, especially an activity or experience. Presume: To expect or assume especially with confidence.

questions.question: Choose the correct meaning of idiom: Bet one's bottom dollar
options: to be unable to afford something that everyone else can
questions.explanation: The idiom 'bet one's bottom dollar' means to be certain about something. E.g. I can bet my bottom dollar that your husband is having an affair with his secretary, as I overheard them talking. Option (b) is the right answer.

questions.question: Why does the passage emphasise that "simple actions, when neglected" lead to larger issues?
options: Because small acts accumulate to create broader disorder and inconvenience
Because individuals intentionally ignore rules in highly crowded spaces
Because public authorities fail to monitor behaviour in shared areas
Because infrastructure in public places is generally insufficient
questions.explanation: Reference sentence: "Simple actions, when neglected, can lead to widespread inconvenience and disorder." The phrase indicates a cumulative effect, where minor negligence builds into larger societal problems. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: What is implied by the phrase "expecting others to clean up after them" in the context of littering?
options: People assume that waste management systems are highly efficient
People lack awareness about the availability of dustbins in public areas
People shift responsibility onto others instead of acting responsibly themselves
People believe cleanliness is solely the duty of municipal authorities
questions.explanation: Reference sentence: "People often throw wrappers.. carelessly, expecting others to clean up after them." The phrase implies a tendency to avoid personal responsibility and rely on others. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Why does the passage group behaviours like loud phone conversations, playing music, and excessive honking together?
options: To show that such actions are common in both rural and urban areas
To highlight that these behaviours collectively disturb shared public environments
To indicate that these actions are legally restricted in most regions
To compare different types of noise pollution in public settings
questions.explanation: Reference phrase: "Loud conversations on phones.. honking excessively on roads further disturb public peace." These behaviours are grouped to show their shared impact on disturbing public peace. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: What underlying idea is conveyed by including both traffic violations and misuse of public property in the same passage?
options: Civic sense is limited only to maintaining cleanliness in public places
Public behaviour is shaped primarily by strict legal enforcement
Civic sense extends across different aspects of shared responsibility
Traffic-related issues are more serious than damage to public property
questions.explanation: Reference phrases: references to "misuse of public property" and "how people follow traffic rules." By including varied examples, the passage broadens civic sense beyond one domain, showing it applies to multiple aspects of public behaviour. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: What is suggested by the sequence in which examples move from visible actions (like littering) to smaller habits (like wasting water)?
options: Major violations are more common than minor acts in public spaces
Civic negligence exists only in highly populated urban environments
The passage prioritises environmental issues over behavioural concerns
Both obvious and subtle actions equally reflect lack of civic awareness
questions.explanation: Reference sentence: "Even small gestures.. indicate negligence." The shift from major to minor examples suggests that all forms of behaviour, whether obvious or subtle, reflect civic sense. Hence, (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Choose the correct antonym of "ARRAIGN":
questions.explanation: To "arraign" is to charge formally or bring before a court. "Acquit" is the correct antonym, as it means to free from a criminal charge or declare innocent. "Indict", "accuse", and "censure" all carry the same sense of blame or condemnation. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: 'Immigiration' is the incorrectly spelt word. The correct spelling is 'immigration' and it means 'the action of coming to live permanently in a foreign country'. Hence, the correct answer is option (c).

questions.question: Choose the correct meaning of the idiom "SPRING TO MIND".
options: To come quickly into your mind
questions.explanation: The idiom 'spring to mind' means 'come to mind quickly'. Hence, the answer is option c).

questions.question: Choose the sentence which uses the adjective in its superlative degree correctly.
options: This was the most clearer route through the old market before the barricades were moved.
This was the clearest route through the old market before the barricades were moved.
This was clearest route through the old market before the barricades were moved.
This was the clearer route through the old market before the barricades were moved.
questions.explanation: The sentence identifies one route as standing above all alternatives, so THE CLEAREST ROUTE is the correct form. Since "clear" is a short adjective, its superlative is made by adding "-est," not by using "most." Rule-based deduction: for many short adjectives, we use "the + adjective-est," as in "the smallest room." Hence, (b) is the correct answer. • Option (a): MOST CLEARER wrongly combines two degree markers. • Option (c): CLEAREST ROUTE needs the article "the" in this structure. • Option (d): CLEARER is comparative, so it does not fit a many-item comparison.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of onomatopoeia?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is a) Hiss. Onomatopoeia is a figure of speech where a word imitates the natural sound of the thing it describes. The word "hiss" phonetically mimics the sound made by a snake or by escaping air. b) Snake: This is the name of an animal. c) Slither: This describes the movement of a snake but does not imitate a sound. d) Green: This is the name of a colour.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into three segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. If the sentence is error free, mark 'No error' as your answer. The stones were bringing to the site / of construction from the neighbouring county / by a delegation of 150 people.
options: of construction from the neighbouring county
The stones were bringing to the site
questions.explanation: The verb 'were bringing' is in active voice which indicates that the subject 'stones' is doing the work that cannot be true. In the given sentence, we should use the passive voice as the verb 'bring' is acted up on the subject 'stones'. Therefore, the passive voice 'were brought' [helping verb + past participle verb (V3)] should be used here. Therefore, the error is in the part in option (d). Hence, option (d) is the correct answer. The correct sentence is: The stones were brought to the site of construction from the neighbouring county by a delegation of 150 people. Note: Voice of a sentence - Depending upon the emphasis we want to lay we use a particular voice in a particular context. We use active voice to lay emphasis on the doer of the action while we use passive voice to lay emphasis on the receiver of the action. Example: Active- Radha will work. (emphasis on Radha) Passive- Work will be done by Radha. (emphasis on Work)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution required'. The thief made by with a bag full of cash.
questions.explanation: "Made by with" is not a valid and meaningful phrase. The correct phrase should be "made away with", which means to steal something and escape. Made away with - Correctly conveys that the thief stole a bag full of cash and ran away. Made from - Incorrect, as it refers to the material something is composed of (e.g., "This table is made from wood"). Made up for - Means to compensate for something, which is unrelated to the context. Thus, the correct substitution is "made away with." The correct sentence is: The thief made away with a bag full of cash. Hence, option (a) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. They spend their own fortune and the fortune of their families.
options: Their own fortune and the fortune of their families are spent by they.
Their own fortune and the fortune of their families were spend by them.
Their own fortune and the fortune of their families are spent by them.
Their families' own fortune and the fortune of them have been spent by they.
questions.explanation: The subject of the Active Voice is changed into the object of the Passive Voice. Also, the object of the Active Voice is changed into the subject of the Passive Voice. The usage of a "to be" form of verb is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Active to Passive. In Passive Voice, the preposition "by" is used before the object. However, it is optional in some cases. The Active voice in this question is in Present Indefinite. Thus, the Passive voice will also be in Present Indefinite. The format of Active voice is: Subject + V1/V5 + Object. Thus, in Passive voice, the format will be: Object of the active voice (Subjective case) + am/is/are + V3 + by + Subject of the active voice (Objective case). For example: She likes you. (Active Voice) You are liked by her. (Passive Voice) The options except (c) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Passive Voice. Therefore, the correct answer is (c): Their own fortune and the fortune of their families are spent by them.

questions.question: Choose the correct meaning of the phrase. In the hollow of your hand.
questions.explanation: The phrase 'in the hollow of your hand' means entirely in your power. Usage- At this point, these men held the entirety of Europe in the hollow of their hands. Hence, (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which spelling is correct for a perfect example of a particular quality?
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is d) Epitome. The epitome of something is a person or thing that is a perfect example of a particular quality or type.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: ANATHEMA
questions.explanation: Anathema - something or someone that one vehemently dislikes or loathes; a curse. Abomination - a thing that causes disgust or hatred. Both words refer to something that is intensely detested or cursed. Hence, option b) is the correct answer. Blessing - a prayer asking for divine favor and protection; a beneficial thing for which one is grateful. Anthem - a rousing or uplifting song identified with a particular group, body, or cause. - a means of solving a problem or dealing with a difficult situation.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in reported speech. Anita said, "As I looked back to bid him goodbye, he was gone."
options: Anita said that as she had looked back to bid him goodbye, he had been gone.
Anita said that as she looked back to bid him goodbye, he had been gone.
Anita said that as she had looked back to bid him goodbye, he was gone.
Anita said that as she looked back to bid him goodbye, he was gone.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (d) Anita said that as she looked back to bid him goodbye, he was gone. Key points: ·If the sentence is in Simple Past, the form of tense doesn't change when it is used in the time clause. ·One way of identifying the time clause is that the Time clause always begins with the following: o When o Whenever o While o As o Since o After o Before o Until o As soon as ·For example: o Ayush said to me, "When I saw him, he was working" o Ayush told me that when he saw him, he was working. ·The following things in the sentence will change when changed from Direct speech to Indirect Speech:Direct SpeechIndirect Speech saidsaid that Ishe ·Therefore, the correct answer is: Anita said that as she looked back to bid him goodbye, he was gone.

questions.question: In literary criticism, the use of a brief reference to a famous person, event, place, or text without explicit explanation is called:
questions.explanation: The crucial clue is "a brief reference" made "without explicit explanation." ALLUSION is the precise literary term for such an indirect reference, so Option (a) is correct. The rule-based deduction is to select the word connected with suggestive reference in literature, not deception, escape, or secret cooperation. • Option (b) ILLUSION means a false appearance or deceptive impression. • Option (c) ELUSION means the act of escaping or avoiding. • Option (d) COLLUSION means secret cooperation for a dishonest purpose.

questions.question: In the following question, the first and the last parts of the sentence/passage are numbered 1 and 6. The rest of the sentence/passage is split into four parts P, Q, R, and S. These four parts are not given in their proper order. Read the sentence/passage and find the most logical order of the parts between 1 and 6. A volcanic eruption is a powerful display of the Earth's inner forces. P. Deep beneath the surface, molten rock known as magma gathers in a magma chamber. Q. When it finally reaches the surface, it erupts as lava, ash, and volcanic gases. R. Gases within this magma begin to expand, which creates immense upward pressure. S. This tremendous pressure then forces the magma to move up through conduits in the rock. 6) The aftermath of such an event can drastically alter the surrounding landscape for many years.
questions.explanation: The passage explains the process of a volcanic eruption in a clear cause-and-effect sequence. 1 introduces the topic. P describes the first stage of the process, which happens "Deep beneath the surface" with the accumulation of magma. R explains the next step: the buildup of pressure within the magma mentioned in P. S describes the direct result of the pressure mentioned in R. "This tremendous pressure" forces the magma upwards. Q describes the final event in the sequence, which is the eruption that happens when the magma from S "finally reaches the surface." 6 concludes by describing the long-term consequences of the eruption. Thus, the correct and logical sequence is PRSQ. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option for the given blank. The ______ the cargo racks were labelled, the faster the inspection team isolated the damaged consignments.
questions.explanation: Option (a) is correct because this sentence follows the correlative comparative pattern: THE MORE ..., THE FASTER .... Since "were labelled" is a verb phrase, the blank needs an adverb, and MORE SYSTEMATICALLY fits perfectly. Rule: in patterns like "the more..., the better..." we use comparative forms on both sides. • Option (b): MOST SYSTEMATICALLY is superlative, but the structure requires a comparative. • Option (c): AS SYSTEMATICALLY belongs to an equality pattern, not this paired comparative structure. • Option (d): SYSTEMATIC is an adjective, but the verb phrase here requires an adverb.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option for the given blank. Despite receiving the data several hours later, the audit desk produced a ______ error log than the field team, which had misread two entries.
questions.explanation: Option (c) is correct because MORE ACCURATE is the proper comparative form used when two entities are directly compared with THAN. Here, the audit desk is being compared with the field team, so the rule requires the comparative degree, not the positive or superlative degree. • Option (a) uses a superlative form, which is used when comparing one with more than two. • Option (b) is a positive-degree pattern and normally works with "as ... as," not with THAN. • Option (d) is also superlative and does not fit a two-item comparison.

questions.question: Choose the correct one-word substitute. An earlier event that is regarded as an example or guide for the future
questions.explanation: Precedent - An earlier event or action that is regarded as an example or guide to be considered in subsequent similar circumstances. Antecedent - A thing that existed before or logically precedes another. Descendent - Descending from an ancestor. Transcendent - Beyond or above the range of normal or physical human experience. So, (d) is the right answer.
